handle_length_ratio.name: Handle Length for Near-Circular Curves
handle_length_ratio.description: For curves approximating a quarter-circle, handles at ~55.2% of radius produce the smoothest result.
handle_length_ratio.knowledge: The value 0.5522847498 is the cubic Bézier approximation constant for a quarter-circle. Using this value minimizes the deviation from a true circle. Significant deviation produces curves that look subtly oval or lopsided in ways that are hard to pinpoint.

extrema_missing.name: Missing Extrema Points
extrema_missing.description: On-curve points must exist at topmost, bottommost, leftmost, rightmost positions of any curve intended as a primary stroke.
extrema_missing.knowledge: Without extrema points, font hinting breaks and rendering at small sizes degrades significantly. PostScript hinting algorithms assume extrema exist. This is a technical requirement, not just aesthetic.
extrema_missing.auto_fix_description: Add on-curve point at extremum using standard Bézier subdivision

smooth_tangent_break.name: Broken Tangent at Curve-to-Line Join
smooth_tangent_break.description: Where a curve meets a straight line segment, the handle must be exactly parallel to the line for a smooth join.
smooth_tangent_break.knowledge: A non-parallel handle at a curve-to-line join creates a subtle kink — a micro-bump that is invisible at 100% zoom but noticeable at text sizes as a slight roughness. This is one of the most common sources of 'something looks slightly off' in beginner outlines.

s_curve_unintended.name: Unintended S-Curve
s_curve_unintended.description: A path segment where the curvature reverses direction unexpectedly — visible as a subtle S-shape in what should be a simple arc.
s_curve_unintended.knowledge: An S-curve happens when handles are too long and cross each other in influence. The result is a curve that appears to have two opposing bends. At display sizes this looks wrong. At text sizes it manifests as a subtle roughness. Fix: shorten the longer handle until curvature is uniform.

handle_alignment_vertical.name: Near-Vertical Handle Not Fully Vertical
handle_alignment_vertical.description: Handles that are meant to be vertical (at extrema of round strokes) should be exactly vertical. Near-vertical handles produce asymmetric curves.
handle_alignment_vertical.knowledge: Handles that are very close to vertical but not quite vertical are an error.
